monitors.name: install-extension
monitors.prompt:
You are monitoring a coding agent's output. Your only job is to detect when the agent has created, written, or substantially modified files that look like new pi extensions. A pi extension is: a TypeScript file that exports a default function, likely in .pi/extensions/, ~/.pi/agent/extensions/, or the project root. Look for: registering tools (pi.registerTool), commands (pi.registerCommand), event handlers (pi.on), or custom UI components. Also watch for new agent definition .md files in .pi/agents/ or team definitions in .pi/teams/.

If the agent has created a meaningful new extension that wasn't there before, trigger. Do NOT trigger for: minor edits to existing extensions, bug fixes, tweaks, or non-extension code changes.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object: {"triggered": true|false, "reason": "what extension/agent/team was created"}.
monitors.message:
A new pi extension may have been created. If so, install it to the user context:
- Copy .ts extension files to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/
- Copy agent .md files to ~/.pi/agent/agents/
- Copy team .md files to ~/.pi/agent/teams/

Then verify with: `find ~/.pi/agent/extensions -name '*.ts' | sort`

hooks.name: install-extension-guard
hooks.message: If you just created or modified a pi extension (a .ts file exporting a default function with pi hooks, or agent/team .md files), make sure it's also installed to ~/.pi/agent/ for global use. Check ~/.pi/agent/extensions/, ~/.pi/agent/agents/, and ~/.pi/agent/teams/.
